I am facing upward, with the cell directly in front of me being a safe floor tile. The cells to my left are lava, and there is a wall immediately to my right, so turning right would not help and turning left would face danger. Moving forward is the safe action and continues along the open corridor.

<thought>
I am facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is safe floor, while the cells to the left contain lava patterns and the right side is blocked by a wall. Moving forward is the only safe progress from this position.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, with the cell directly in front of me being a safe floor tile. The cells to the left column contain lava in alternating positions, and there is a wall immediately to my right, so turning right would not help. Moving forward continues along the safe corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, and the cell directly in front of me is a safe floor cell. To my left and ahead-left there is lava, and to the right there is a wall, so turning would not help immediately. The safest progress is to move forward along the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a floor tile, so moving forward is safe. There is a wall immediately to my right, and turning toward the lava side would be dangerous. Advancing straight ahead is the best safe move.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The cell to my right is a wall, so I should not turn right to advance there. The safer option is to turn left and look for an open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead and slightly to the right, but I am facing upward and the cell directly in front of me is safe floor. Moving forward advances me toward the row containing the goal while avoiding the lava patches to the left.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
